messages.content:
You are the ComfyUI agent in a sidebar panel, driving the user's live ComfyUI graph and server. Answer in normal Markdown.

You have exactly six tools:
- list_tools / describe_tool / call_tool — the headless ComfyUI server (~200 capabilities: generate images/video/audio, models, custom nodes, queue, diagnostics). Flow: list_tools {"search": ...} → describe_tool {"name": ...} → call_tool {"name": ..., "args": {...}}.
- panel_list_tools / panel_describe_tool / panel_call_tool — the user's LIVE canvas (read the graph, add/wire nodes, set widgets, run, screenshots, show media). Same flow.

Rules:
- Catalog entries are tool NAMES, not data. Finish every task by actually running tools; never invent results.
- Describe a tool before its first call so you use the right parameters. If a call errors, read the error — it includes the expected schema — fix the args and retry.
- To see or show any generated image/video, run the panel_show_media tool via panel_call_tool.
- Workflows with API nodes cost the user PAID credits; local-GPU workflows are free. Ask before anything that might spend credits.
Did my last run hit any errors? Check and tell me.

comfyui-mcp tool catalog — 23 of 113 tools (filtered). Workflow: pick a tool → describe_tool {"name": ...} for its parameters → call_tool {"name": ..., "args": {...}}.
## workflows (4)
- rerun_generation: Re-run the workflow behind a previous generation.
- get_system_stats: Get system information from the connected ComfyUI server: GPU device(s), total/free VRAM, ComfyUI/Python/PyTorch versions, and OS details.
- get_job_status: Check the status of ONE ComfyUI job by its prompt_id (the id returned by enqueue_workflow).
- run_workflow_url: Read (and optionally execute) a shared ComfyUI workflow from a URL.

## workflow-authoring (2)
- validate_workflow: Validate a ComfyUI workflow without executing it.
- save_workflow: Save a workflow JSON to the connected ComfyUI server's user library so it appears in the ComfyUI web UI.

## custom-nodes (7)
- install_custom_node: Install a ComfyUI custom node pack by registry id (e.g.
- update_custom_node: Update an installed ComfyUI custom node pack, or pass 'all' to update every installed pack.
- reinstall_custom_node: Reinstall a ComfyUI custom node pack (uninstall then install).
- fix_custom_node: Repair a ComfyUI custom node pack's install and Python dependencies, or pass 'all' to repair every pack.
- list_installed_nodes: List installed ComfyUI custom node packs with their version and enabled/disabled state.
- sync_node_dependencies: Reconcile the Python dependencies of all installed custom node packs (comfy-cli `node uv-sync` analogue).
- verify_custom_node: Test that a custom-node pack actually loads in ComfyUI — the middle step of the author loop (scaffold_custom_node → verify_custom_node → publish_custom_node).

## diagnostics (4)
- get_logs: Get ComfyUI server runtime logs.
- get_history: Get execution history for a ComfyUI prompt.
- report_issue: Build a ready-to-open GitHub issue link for a bug/problem you hit (ComfyUI, a workflow, a model, custom nodes, or comfyui-mcp itself).
- health_check: Pre-flight diagnostic for the connected ComfyUI: one call that aggregates the signals an agent should check before dispatching a batch.

## server (3)
- update_comfyui: Update the ComfyUI core install: runs `git pull` in the configured ComfyUI directory and reinstalls its Python requirements (auto-detecting uv vs pip).
- install_panel: Install, update, reinstall, or report status of the ComfyUI sidebar panel ('comfyui-agent-panel' on the Comfy Registry; repo comfyui-mcp-panel) in the LOCAL Co…
- self_update: Check or apply a self-update of the comfyui-mcp npm package against the npm registry.

## generation (3)
- generate_video: Generate a short video from a text prompt (text-to-video), or animate a start image (image-to-video when `image` is given) — the high-level entry point.
- remove_background: Remove an image's background, returning a transparent (RGBA) cutout — the high-level entry point.
- upscale_image: Upscale an image with an ESRGAN super-resolution model — the high-level entry point.

This is a FILTERED view (90 tools hidden). If nothing here fits the task, call list_tools again with a broader search or no filter.

Yes — your last run hit **validation errors** (the same set repeated a few times). Here's the breakdown:

---

### 1. 🔴 `DualCLIPLoader 2` — Model file not found
> **`qwen_4b_ace15.safetensors`** is not in the list of available clip models.

The available options are: `ernie-image-prompt-enhancer`, `gemma4_e4b_it_fp8_scaled`, `gemma_3_12B_it_fp4_mixed`, `gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled`, `ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16`, `ministral-3-3b`, `qwen3vl_4b_fp8_scaled`, `qwen3vl_8b_fp8_scaled`, `qwen_3_06b_base`, `umt5-xxl-enc-bf16`, `umt5_xxl_fp8_e4m3fn_scaled`.

You'll need to either **download** `qwen_4b_ace15.safetensors` into your CLIP models folder, or **switch** to one of the listed models.

---

### 2. 🔴 `UNETLoader 1` — Missing input
> Required input **`unet_name`** is not connected/set.

The UNET loader has no model selected. You need to wire a UNET file name into this node.

---

### 3. 🔴 `TextEncodeAceStepAudio1.5 6` — Many missing inputs
> Required inputs are missing: `generate_audio_codes`, `timesignature`, `keyscale`, `cfg_scale`, `temperature`, `bpm`, `top_p`, `min_p`, `top_k`, `tags`.

This text-encoder node has a lot of required parameters that aren't wired up.

---

### 4. 🔴 `SaveAudioMP3 8 & 10` / Prompt root — Missing `quality`
> Required input **`quality`** is missing on `SaveAudioMP3` nodes (and propagated to the prompt level).

Both audio save nodes need a `quality` parameter set (e.g., `128`, `192`, `320`).

---

### Summary
The workflow **failed validation and never actually executed**. It looks like an Ace-Step audio generation workflow that's missing:
- A valid CLIP model file
- A UNET model selection  
- Multiple required audio-encoding parameters
- MP3 quality settings

Want me to take a look at your live canvas and help fix these nodes?
